message:
fcp-bootstrap/hardware_token: fail-closed on empty PIN before any PKCS#11 operation

The PKCS#11 session driver and its callers would forward an empty
`HardwareTokenPin` straight into `session.login_with_pin()`, the
enumeration session constructor, and the certificate-selection helper.
Depending on the underlying PKCS#11 module, an empty PIN might be
silently accepted (on tokens that expose "CKF_PROTECTED_AUTHENTICATION_PATH"
and interpret empty pin as "use protected auth path"), might return a
generic `CKR_PIN_INCORRECT`, or might lock the token after repeated
attempts. None of those behaviours are the ones we want from the
fcp-bootstrap layer, which expects the operator to have supplied a
real PIN before any probe is performed.

Fix: guard every entry point that takes `&HardwareTokenPin` with an
`if pin.is_empty() { return Err(TokenError::PinRequired); }` up
front, before any PKCS#11 side-effect (lock acquisition, session
open, login attempt, object enumeration).

Changes in crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:

* Add the empty-PIN guard to:
  - `Pkcs11SessionDriver::authenticate`
  - `Pkcs11SessionDriver::enumerate_certificates`
  - `Pkcs11SessionDriver::enumerate_keys`
  - `open_enumeration_session` (internal helper shared by both
    enumerators)
  - `select_certificate_for_provisioning` (trait-driver-agnostic
    top-level helper used by the bootstrap flow)
* Add `select_cert_empty_pin_returns_pin_required` regression test:
  constructs a MockSessionDriver with one cert + one Ed25519 key,
  passes `HardwareTokenPin::new("")`, asserts the result is
  `Err(TokenError::PinRequired)` (i.e. the guard fires before the
  driver is even consulted — no certificates enumerated, no keys
  enumerated, no token lockout risk).

The underlying driver trait `HardwareTokenSessionDriver` is
unchanged; real drivers and mocks continue to accept a
`&HardwareTokenPin`, and the guard sits in front of them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
